items.title: Machines of Loving Grace
items.author: Dario Amodei
items.description: Amodei's long-form vision of how transformative AI could compress decades of scientific progress into a few years — covering biology, neuroscience, mental health, economic development and more. Essential reading on AI's potential upside.

items.title: On the Importance of AI Safety Research
items.author: Dario Amodei
items.description: Anthropic's CEO on why safety and capability research must proceed together, not in sequence. The clearest articulation of the 'race to the top' philosophy from inside the frontier lab.

items.title: Claude's Model Spec (Constitutional AI)
items.author: Dario Amodei
items.description: The specification document that defines how Claude is trained to be helpful, harmless, and honest. A remarkable piece of AI alignment engineering made public.

items.title: Planning for AGI and Beyond
items.author: Sam Altman
items.description: OpenAI's CEO lays out the organisation's transition plan for when AGI arrives — governance structures, iterative deployment, and why 'slow is impossible'. Candid and worth reading critically.

items.title: The Intelligence Age
items.author: Sam Altman
items.description: Altman's argument that we're entering a new era of human prosperity driven by AI tools accessible to everyone — optimistic, controversial, and impossible to ignore.

items.title: Intro to Large Language Models (lecture)
items.author: Andrej Karpathy
items.description: One of the clearest technical explanations of LLMs ever recorded. Karpathy walks from tokenisation through attention to RLHF. Mandatory viewing for anyone working in this space.

items.title: Let's Build GPT from Scratch
items.author: Andrej Karpathy
items.description: Karpathy builds a GPT implementation in ~2 hours of live coding. The single best way to understand what a transformer actually is. Code is clear, explanations are exceptional.

items.title: The Spelled-Out Intro to Neural Networks & Backprop
items.author: Andrej Karpathy
items.description: Micrograd: building autograd from scratch with pure Python. The deepest, most rigorous treatment of backpropagation I've seen in video format. Essential before you touch PyTorch.

items.title: Software 2.0
items.author: Andrej Karpathy
items.description: Karpathy's prescient argument that neural networks are not just a tool but a new programming paradigm — one where the programmer specifies desired behaviour, not explicit logic. Reads even better today.

items.title: The Forward-Forward Algorithm (2022)
items.author: Geoffrey Hinton
items.description: Hinton's alternative to backpropagation — training neural networks using only local, positive and negative data without ever computing a global gradient. Potentially significant for neuromorphic and on-device learning.

items.title: Geoffrey Hinton — Turing Award Lecture
items.author: Geoffrey Hinton
items.description: Hinton's lecture on the development of deep learning, from the early days of backpropagation through the ImageNet moment. A first-hand account of the long AI winter and what it took to survive it.

items.title: Will Digital Intelligence Replace Biological Intelligence?
items.author: Geoffrey Hinton
items.description: Hinton's talk following his departure from Google — the 'Godfather of AI' explains why he changed his mind on AI risk, and what he now believes we should be worried about.

items.title: Attention Is All You Need
items.author: Vaswani et al. (Google Brain)
items.description: The paper that changed everything. The transformer architecture introduced here is the foundation of every major language model in existence. If you work in AI, you must have read this.

items.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
items.author: Kaplan et al. (OpenAI)
items.description: The empirical laws governing how model performance scales with compute, data, and parameters. The theoretical backbone behind the scaling hypothesis that has driven frontier AI for five years.

items.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
items.author: Bai et al. (Anthropic)
items.description: The research paper behind Claude's training methodology. Describes how a set of principles ('constitution') guides RLHF without requiring human labellers for every harmful output.

items.title: Deep Learning (textbook)
items.author: Goodfellow, Bengio, Courville
items.description: The canonical deep learning textbook — freely available online. Chapter 6 onwards remains the most rigorous treatment of feedforward nets, regularisation, and optimisation available.
